title: Abstracts
generation_instructions: Write three abstracts (English, Hebrew, and Layman's terms) each limited to one page. Each abstract must include: background and aim, methods, expected results, importance to medicine, and five keywords. Ensure the English abstract is clear and concise, suitable for a broad scientific audience. The Hebrew abstract should accurately reflect the content of the English abstract. The Layman's terms abstract should be easily understandable by a non-scientific audience, focusing on the potential impact and relevance of the research.
search_queries: abstract guidelines for medical research grants
how to write a layman's abstract
translating scientific abstracts into plain language

title: Research Plan
generation_instructions: Develop a comprehensive research plan, up to 10 pages, excluding references. Include the following sections: scientific background, clearly stating the research question; detailed study design and methods, including sample size justification and available equipment; preliminary results; a schematic representation of the project with responsibilities and a time scheme; and a time estimate for each stage. Ensure the plan is well-organized, scientifically sound, and clearly demonstrates the feasibility of the proposed research.
search_queries: writing a research plan for grant proposal
elements of a strong research plan
